Defendants Minted Llc, Vineet Gupta, And Carly Smith'S Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Sep-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
John David, Esq. (310.860.0770; JDavid@Shegerianlaw.com) of Shegerian & Associates, Inc. for Plaintiff.
Carl Kaplan, Esq. (650.265.0222; ckaplan@svelf.com) of SV Employment Law Firm PC for Defendants.

ARGUED The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS MINTED LLC, VINEET GUPTA and CARLY SMITH'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action:

Defendants Minted Llc, Vineet Gupta, And Carly Smith's Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is denied.

"The petitioner bears the burden of proving the existence of a valid arbitration agreement by the preponderance of the evidence, and a party opposing the petition bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to its defense." (Engalla v. Permanente Medical Group Inc. (1997) 15 Cal.4th 951, 972. Here, defendant does not establish that the parties entered into an arbitration agreement and plaintiff has established defenses.

Defendant may have met its burden on the first step by attaching to Bailey's declaration, copies of the arbitration agreements purporting to bear Plaintiff's signature. However, Plaintiff likewise met her burden on the second step by filing an opposing declaration, saying she did not electronically sign the agreements and would not have signed the agreements and waived her rights. (See Ruiz v. Moss Bros. Auto Group, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 836, 846 [Petitioner having no recall of signing arbitration agreement placed the burden on Defendant to prove by a preponderance of evidence that the electronic signature was authentic].) Christina Bailey's declaration is devoid of preliminary facts that demonstrate personal knowledge of what she attested to. Bailey's boilerplate sentence that she has "personal knowledge of the facts set forth herein, except as to those matters stated on information and belief, and if called upon as a witness, I could and would competently testify," (see Bailey Decl.) is not sufficient to establish personal knowledge. (See Gamboa v. Northeast Cmty. Clinic, (2021) 72 Cal.App.5th 158, 169 & fn.4 [boilerplate language alone is insufficient to establish personal knowledge.].)

Defendant's supplemental filings fail to cure the defect because there is no authentication for the electronic signatures. Rather, the supplemental filings discuss company policy and procedures for how employees sign documents using applications like DocuSign and Sapling. Further, Defendant alleges that the May 2019 agreement was sent to Plaintiff's personal email (Supplemental Bailey Decl., Ex. A) however, the audit trail for the May 2019 agreement is listed as being from Plaintiff's work email (Bailey Decl., Ex. 2) and no explanation was provided as to why. (Snider v. Snider (1962) 200 Cal.App.2d 741, 754 [" 'Where the facts stated do not themselves show it, such bare statement of the affiant has no redeeming value and should be ignored.' "].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
